title: Sphaerocephalus subglaber
intro: Sphaerocephalus subglaber, also known as Lesser Club Moss or Sphagnum Club Moss, is a type of ancient, non-vascular plant that belongs to the Sphagnopsida division. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, skin conditions, and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Sphaerocephalus subglaber include phenolic compounds, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support respiratory health, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing.
